title: Stay Sharp: - Take Notes: Write Down Key Points from the Person Speaking Before
subtitle: Focus During Your Turn in Groups
description:
To stay sharp:
- Take notes: Write down key points from the person speaking before you.
- Breathe and listen: Avoid rehearsing your own response while someone else is speaking.
- Repeat mentally: After someone speaks, quickly repeat their main point in your head.

Example: In a team meeting, note what the person before you says and reference it when it’s your turn.
keywords: Focus During Your Turn in Groups

howTo.title: Define the metric baseline
howTo.summary: Identify your current listening habits by tracking how often you engage in active listening during meetings. Note the frequency of clarifying questions and follow-up emails.

howTo.title: Implement the micro-note ritual
howTo.summary: During conversations, write down 1 to 5 words summarizing the previous speaker's main point. Breathe for a few seconds and mentally repeat their point before responding.

howTo.title: Practice in varied contexts
howTo.summary: Use the micro-note ritual in different settings, such as team meetings, one-on-ones, and casual conversations. Experiment with different note types: keywords, frames, and sentences.

howTo.title: Review and adjust your approach
howTo.summary: After a week, assess how the micro-note ritual impacted your clarity and engagement. Adjust your approach based on what worked and what didn’t.

howTo.title: Check in with LifeOS Brali app
howTo.summary: Log your results and reflections in the Brali LifeOS app to track your progress and refine your active listening skills. Visit https://metalhatscats.com/life-os/active-listening-meeting-notes/.
